USGS.WaterQuality.Sites.MonitoringSite.name: MonitoringSite
USGS.WaterQuality.Sites.MonitoringSite.description: USGS water quality monitoring site reference data. Describes a physical monitoring location equipped with continuous water quality sensors.

USGS.WaterQuality.Readings.WaterQualityR.name: WaterQualityReading
USGS.WaterQuality.Readings.WaterQualityR.description: A single water quality observation from a USGS continuous monitoring sensor, including dissolved oxygen, pH, water temperature, specific conductance, turbidity, and nitrate readings.

schema.name: MonitoringSite
schema.description: USGS water quality monitoring site. Describes a physical surface-water or groundwater monitoring location equipped with continuous water quality sensors. Site metadata is extracted from the WaterML 2.0 JSON response sourceInfo block returned by the USGS Instantaneous Values Service.

fields.name: site_number
fields.description: USGS site identification number. A unique 8-to-15-digit number assigned by the USGS to each monitoring location (e.g. '01646500').

fields.name: site_name
fields.description: Official USGS station name describing the monitoring location (e.g. 'POTOMAC RIVER NEAR WASH, DC LITTLE FALLS PUMP STA').

fields.name: agency_code
fields.description: Code identifying the agency responsible for the site. Typically 'USGS' for United States Geological Survey.

fields.name: latitude
fields.description: Decimal latitude of the monitoring site in the WGS84 (EPSG:4326) coordinate reference system.

fields.name: longitude
fields.description: Decimal longitude of the monitoring site in the WGS84 (EPSG:4326) coordinate reference system.

fields.name: site_type
fields.description: USGS site type code indicating the category of the monitoring location. Common values include 'ST' (stream), 'LK' (lake), 'GW' (groundwater), 'ES' (estuary).

fields.name: state_code
fields.description: Two-digit FIPS state code for the state or territory where the site is located (e.g. '24' for Maryland).

fields.name: county_code
fields.description: Five-digit FIPS county code for the county where the site is located (e.g. '24031' for Montgomery County, MD).

fields.name: huc_code
fields.description: Hydrologic Unit Code (HUC) identifying the watershed or drainage basin containing the site.

schema.name: WaterQualityReading
schema.description: A single water quality observation from a USGS continuous monitoring sensor. The USGS Instantaneous Values Service returns time series of sensor readings in WaterML 2.0 JSON format. Each reading represents one measurement at a specific date_time for one parameter at one site.

fields.name: site_number
fields.description: USGS site identification number for the monitoring location that produced this reading (e.g. '01646500').

fields.name: site_name
fields.description: Official USGS station name for the monitoring location.

fields.name: parameter_code
fields.description: Five-digit USGS parameter code identifying the measured quantity. Key water quality codes: 00010 (water temperature °C), 00300 (dissolved oxygen mg/L), 00400 (pH), 00095 (specific conductance µS/cm), 63680 (turbidity FNU), 99133 (nitrate+nitrite mg/L as N).

fields.name: parameter_name
fields.description: Human-readable name of the measured parameter as provided in the USGS variable description (e.g. 'Temperature, water, degrees Celsius').

fields.name: value
fields.description: Numeric sensor reading for the parameter at the given date_time. Null when the sensor did not return a valid numeric value (e.g. equipment malfunction, ice-affected, or the USGS noDataValue sentinel -999999.0).

fields.name: unit
fields.description: Unit of measurement for the reading as reported by the USGS (e.g. 'deg C', 'mg/l', 'uS/cm @25C', 'FNU', 'mg/l as N').

fields.name: qualifier
fields.description: USGS data qualifier code indicating the quality or status of the reading. Common values: 'P' (provisional), 'A' (approved), 'e' (estimated). Null when no qualifier is provided.

fields.name: date_time
fields.description: ISO 8601 date-time string of the observation in UTC (e.g. '2024-11-15T16:45:00+00:00'). Converted from the site-local timestamp using the timezone information provided in the WaterML 2.0 response.
